county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Cincinnati. Ohio's first county (1790, Northwest Territory). Cincinnati is a major genealogical research center — Cincinnati History Library and Archives (formerly Cincinnati Historical Society) holds extensive collections. German immigration center — Over-the-Rhine neighborhood records. Cincinnati Public Library Genealogy and Local History department. Hebrew Union College archives for Jewish families. Catholic Archdiocese of Cincinnati records (from 1821). Court records from 1790. Procter & Gamble and other major employer records. African American community records — Harriet Beecher Stowe House, National Underground Railroad Freedom Center resources.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1790. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1790. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Hamilton County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1790. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Hamilton County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Hamilton County Clerk of Courts, Cincinnati
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1790. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Hamilton County Auditor, Cincinnati / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1790. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

source_profiles.source_name: Symmes Purchase Land Records — Hamilton County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Cincinnati History Library and Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: John Cleves Symmes purchased approximately 1 million acres between the Great Miami and Little Miami Rivers. Records document original patent holders and early settlers in SW Ohio.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records at Ohio History Connection and Cincinnati History Library and Archives

source_profiles.repository: Hamilton County Clerk of Courts, Cincinnati / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Hamilton County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Findlay. Located in Congress Lands. Findlay oil and gas boom (1880s-1890s) brought workers documented in city directories and newspapers. Marathon Oil Company founded here. University of Findlay (1882) archives. Hancock County Historical Society. Strong German Catholic and Lutheran communities. Blanchard River flood records. Check Logan County for pre-1828 organizational records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1820. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Hancock County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1820. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Kenton. Created 1820 from Logan County. Located in Congress Lands. Ohio Northern University (1871) archives in Ada. Hardin County Historical Society. Scioto Marsh drainage project records document land transformation and settlers. Agricultural county — township school and road records important. Strong German and Scots-Irish heritage. Check Logan County for early records. Kenton named for frontiersman Simon Kenton.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Cadiz. Created 1813 from Jefferson and Tuscarawas. Named for William Henry Harrison. Cadiz was the birthplace of Clark Gable. Coal mining heritage — mine employment records document Welsh, Scottish, and Eastern European immigrants. Harrison County Historical Society. Conotton Creek area early settlement. Quaker settlement records in Freeport area. Puskarich Public Library has local history collection.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1813. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Napoleon. Created 1820, organized 1834. Located in the Black Swamp region — late settlement. Miami & Erie Canal passed through — canal construction and operation records. Henry County Historical Society. Strong German heritage — Holgate, Liberty Center, and surrounding communities. Flat terrain meant extensive drainage tile records documenting land improvements. Check Shelby County for pre-1834 records. Napoleon named for Napoleon Bonaparte.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Ohio History Connection

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Hillsboro. Located in the Virginia Military District — VA bounty land warrants essential. Hillsboro was the birthplace of the Women's Temperance Crusade (1873) — temperance movement records. Highland County Historical Society. Underground Railroad activity; numerous documented routes. Strong Quaker presence — Leesburg and other Friends meeting records. Rocky Fork Lake area. Check Ross, Adams, and Clermont for pre-1805 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1805. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1805. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.repository: Highland County Clerk of Courts, Hillsboro
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1805. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Military District Land Warrants — Highland County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Highland County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.source_name: Society of Friends (Quaker) Meeting Records — Highland County
source_profiles.repository: Wilmington College Quaker Heritage Center / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Monthly meeting records include births, marriages, deaths, removals (transfers), and disownments. Quakers kept meticulous records. Ohio Yearly Meeting and Indiana Yearly Meeting records at Wilmington College.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptionally well-preserved; many microfilmed and indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Logan. Created 1818 from Athens, Ross, and Fairfield. Hocking Hills region — early settlement records sparse due to rugged terrain. Hocking County Historical Society. Coal mining and iron furnace records (1830s-1900s). Logan Elm area — site of Shawnee Chief Logan's famous speech. Hocking Canal records. Ohio University Hocking College campus. Check parent counties for pre-1818 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.repository: Hocking County Clerk of Courts, Logan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1818. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1818. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

source_profiles.source_name: Hanging Rock Iron Region Records — Hocking County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Charcoal iron furnace records including worker rolls, company store ledgers, and furnace operation logs. The Hanging Rock Iron Region in southern Ohio employed thousands of workers including Welsh, English, and African American laborers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered — some at Ohio History Connection, some at local museums (e.g., Buckeye Furnace State Memorial)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Millersburg. Largest Amish community in the world — Amish and Mennonite church records, family histories (Hostetler, Miller, Yoder, etc.), and settlement records are essential. Holmes County Historical Society. Created 1824 from Coshocton, Tuscarawas, and Wayne. German-Swiss heritage. Killbuck Creek valley. Amish-related records may be in private family collections rather than public repositories. FamilySearch has some microfilmed records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1824. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1824. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Holmes County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Holmes County Clerk of Courts, Millersburg
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1824. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.source_name: Amish and Mennonite Church Records — Holmes County
source_profiles.repository: Heritage Historical Library, Aylmer ON / Mennonite Historical Library, Goshen IN / Local church records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Amish church district records, family group sheets, Budget newspaper correspondence, and Mennonite church records. Ohio has the largest Amish population in the world. Records may be in German/Pennsylvanian Dutch. Die Botschaft and The Budget newspapers document Amish community events.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Amish records remain in private family collections; Heritage Historical Library has compiled indexes

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Norwalk. Firelands (Sufferers' Lands) area — Connecticut granted these lands to residents whose property was burned by the British in the Revolutionary War. Check Connecticut state archives for fire sufferers claims. Huron County Historical Society. Western Reserve territory. Milan was birthplace of Thomas Edison. Early Huron County included much of modern Erie and part of other counties — check for boundary changes. Norwalk has well-preserved early court records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1809. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Connecticut Western Reserve Land Records — Huron County
source_profiles.repository: Western Reserve Historical Society, Cleveland / CT State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Connecticut ceded its Western Reserve to the federal government in 1800, but settlers arrived from 1796. Connecticut Land Company records document original purchasers and settlers. Check Connecticut state records for settler origins.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at WRHS in Cleveland; Connecticut State Library also has copies

source_profiles.repository: Connecticut State Library / Firelands Historical Society, Norwalk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Firelands were granted by Connecticut to residents whose property was burned by the British during the Revolutionary War. Claims document the original Connecticut property owners and their heirs who received Ohio lands.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Published lists of fire sufferers exist; original records at CT State Library

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1816 from Scioto, Gallia, Athens, Ross.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Jackson. Created 1816 from Scioto, Gallia, Athens, and Ross. Iron furnace heritage — Jackson County had numerous charcoal iron furnaces (1820s-1890s); furnace records document workers including Welsh, English, and African American laborers. Buckeye Furnace State Memorial. Jackson County Historical Society. Located in Congress Lands. Appalachian Ohio — coal mining records in later period. Leo Petroglyphs state memorial area.
